mnemonics: B.I.N.S. for Binomial: Binary outcomes, Independent trials, Number of trials fixed, Same probability of success.
Mean = n*p (Number of trials * Probability of success)
Variance = n*p*q (n * p * q, where q is 1-p)
keyPoints: The binomial distribution models the number of successes in a fixed number of independent Bernoulli trials.
The mean (expected value) of a binomial distribution is μ = n * p.
The variance of a binomial distribution is σ² = n * p * (1 - p), which measures the spread or variability around the mean.
commonMistakes: Confusing the formula for variance (n*p*q) with the formula for the mean (n*p).
Applying the binomial distribution when trials are not independent or the probability of success changes, which violates its assumptions.
